content: Hi, I'm Rowan. I've been really busy lately with work and taking care of my aging parents.
Hi Rowan, nice to meet you. I'm so sorry to hear that you're dealing with a lot right now. How are your parents doing?
They're doing okay, thanks for asking. It's just been a challenge balancing my job as a social worker with their care needs. I feel like I'm being pulled in a million different directions sometimes.
I can imagine. It's great that you're dedicated to your work and your family, but it's also important to take care of yourself. Do you have any self-care routines or activities that help you relax?
Yeah, I actually love playing the piano. It's been a hobby of mine since I was a kid, and it's a great way to unwind after a long day.
That's beautiful. Music can be so therapeutic. Do you have a favorite type of music to play or listen to?
I really love classical music, especially Chopin. There's something about the complexity and emotion of his pieces that I find really compelling.
Chopin is amazing. I've heard his music is really challenging to play, but also very rewarding. Do you have a favorite piece or concerto that you like to play?
Actually, I've been working on his Piano Concerto No. 2. It's a really beautiful piece, and I love the way the piano and orchestra come together.
That sounds stunning. I'm sure it's a lot of work to practice and perfect, but it's great that you're challenging yourself. Do you have any plans to perform or share your music with others?
Yeah, I actually perform occasionally at local events and charities. It's a great way to give back and share my passion with others.

That's really great. Giving back to the community is so important, and it's wonderful that you're using your talents to make a difference. What inspired you to become a social worker, and what do you love most about your job?

I've just always been passionate about helping others, and I felt like social work was a way for me to make a real difference in people's lives. I love working with foster care families and helping them navigate the system.

That's amazing. Foster care is such an important issue, and it's great that you're dedicated to helping those families. Do you have any favorite travel destinations or places you like to visit in your free time?
Actually, I love traveling and exploring new places. I've been to some amazing destinations, like New Zealand and Italy, and I'm always looking for new adventures.
Wow, those sound like incredible trips. I've heard New Zealand is just stunning, with the beautiful landscapes and outdoor activities. What was your favorite part of that trip?

Definitely the scenery and the people. New Zealanders are so friendly and welcoming, and the country is just breathtakingly beautiful. I also loved trying all the local foods and drinks, like flat whites and pavlova.
Mmm, that sounds delicious. I've heard the coffee culture in New Zealand is really great, and the food is amazing. Do you have any favorite foods or restaurants in Raleigh, where you live?
Yeah, I actually love the barbecue here. There's a great restaurant called The Pit that has amazing pulled pork and sides.
Barbecue is a staple of the South, and it's great that you have a favorite spot. Do you have any fun plans or events coming up in the next few months?
Actually, I'm planning to take a trip to Asheville in a few weeks to visit some friends and explore the city. I've heard it's really beautiful and vibrant, with a great arts and culture scene.

Asheville is amazing. I've heard the Blue Ridge Mountains are just stunning, and the city has a really unique energy. You'll have to try some of the local craft beer and visit the River Arts District.
